truth.papers.A2.title: Work hardening in Fe–Al alloys
truth.papers.A2.paper_scope: Examine work hardening in polycrystalline Fe–Al alloys with Al content from 15 to 35 at.%, focusing on dislocation structures, ordering, and elastic anisotropy.

prediction.papers.A2.title: Work hardening in Fe–Al alloys
prediction.papers.A2.paper_scope: Examination of room-temperature work hardening and post-deformation dislocation structures in polycrystalline Fe-Al alloys containing 15–35 at.% Al, focusing on the mechanisms responsible for the sharp peak in work hardening rate near Fe-28% Al.

truth.processes.proc_15al_ac.description: Induction melting under inert gas, drop casting into 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, slow cooling. As-cast condition with coarse grain size ~0.25 mm.
truth.processes.proc_20al_ac.description: Induction melting under inert gas, drop casting into 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, slow cooling. As-cast condition with coarse grain size ~0.25 mm.
truth.processes.proc_25al_ac.description: Induction melting under inert gas, drop casting into 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, slow cooling. As-cast condition with coarse grain size ~0.25 mm, high degree of B2 order and good degree of DO3 order.
truth.processes.proc_28al_ac.description: Induction melting under inert gas, drop casting into 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, slow cooling. As-cast condition with coarse grain size ~0.25 mm, high degree of B2 order and weak DO3 order.
truth.processes.proc_30al_ac.description: Induction melting under inert gas, drop casting into 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, slow cooling. As-cast condition with coarse grain size ~0.25 mm, high degree of B2 order.
truth.processes.proc_35al_ac.description: Induction melting under inert gas, drop casting into 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, slow cooling. As-cast condition with coarse grain size ~0.25 mm, high degree of B2 order.
truth.processes.proc_20al_01b_ac.description: Induction melting under inert gas, drop casting into 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, slow cooling. As-cast condition.
truth.processes.proc_30al_01b_ac.description: Induction melting under inert gas, drop casting into 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, slow cooling. As-cast condition.

prediction.processes.proc_15al_ac.description: Induction melting in alumina crucible under inert gas, drop casting into a 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, followed by sufficiently slow cooling after solidification.
prediction.processes.proc_15al_ac.processes_notes: Grain size of the order of 0.25 mm. No grain size, solution, or particle hardening effects intended.
prediction.processes.proc_20al_ac.description: Induction melting in alumina crucible under inert gas, drop casting into a 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, followed by sufficiently slow cooling after solidification.
prediction.processes.proc_20al_ac.processes_notes: Grain size of the order of 0.25 mm.
prediction.processes.proc_25al_ac.description: Induction melting in alumina crucible under inert gas, drop casting into a 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, followed by sufficiently slow cooling after solidification.
prediction.processes.proc_25al_ac.processes_notes: Slow cooling allows a good degree of DO3 order for the 25–30% Al composition range, with strong superlattice spots and coarse DO3 domains observed by TEM.
prediction.processes.proc_28al_ac.description: Induction melting in alumina crucible under inert gas, drop casting into a 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, followed by sufficiently slow cooling after solidification.
prediction.processes.proc_28al_ac.processes_notes: Slow cooling allows a good degree of DO3 order.
prediction.processes.proc_30al_ac.description: Induction melting in alumina crucible under inert gas, drop casting into a 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, followed by sufficiently slow cooling after solidification.
prediction.processes.proc_30al_ac.processes_notes: Slow cooling allows a high degree of B2 order and weak DO3 order.
prediction.processes.proc_35al_ac.description: Induction melting in alumina crucible under inert gas, drop casting into a 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, followed by sufficiently slow cooling after solidification.
prediction.processes.proc_35al_ac.processes_notes: Slow cooling allows a high degree of B2 order.
prediction.processes.proc_20al_01b_ac.description: Induction melting in alumina crucible under inert gas, drop casting into a 25 mm diameter cylindrical copper mould, followed by sufficiently slow cooling after solidification.
prediction.processes.proc_20al_01b_ac.processes_not: Boron-containing variant; grain size of the order of 0.25 mm.

prediction.processes.proc_30al_01b_ac.processes_not: Boron-containing variant; slow cooling allows a high degree of B2 order.

truth.structures.sample_15al.overall_structure: As-cast polycrystalline disordered bcc solid solution with dislocation substructure.
truth.structures.sample_15al.microstructure_li: Moderate, single dislocations arranged in loose cellular distribution with many dipoles and small loops
truth.structures.sample_20al.overall_structure: As-cast polycrystalline disordered/short-range ordered bcc solid solution with dislocation substructure.
truth.structures.sample_20al.microstructure_li: Moderate, single dislocations, some straight screw dislocations, many edge dipole segments and loops
truth.structures.sample_25al.overall_structure: As-cast polycrystalline B2 ordered matrix with imperfect DO3 order, containing dislocations and APB faults.
truth.structures.sample_25al.microstructure_li: coarse polygonal grains, ~0.25 mm, with coarse B2 domains and fine DO3 domains
Single dislocations (1/2<111>), loosely coupled by weak APB faults, many near-screw and edge dipoles, APBI faults observed
truth.structures.sample_28al.overall_structure: As-cast polycrystalline B2 ordered structure with weak DO3 order, containing superdislocations and sessile locks.
truth.structures.sample_28al.microstructure_li: High density of straight near-screw superdislocations (two 1/2<111> partials), many superdislocation dipoles, sessile <100> edge dislocations formed by reactions
truth.structures.sample_30al.overall_structure: As-cast polycrystalline B2 ordered structure with weak DO3 order, containing superdislocations and APBII faults.
truth.structures.sample_30al.microstructure_li: Straight near-screw superdislocations (two 1/2<111> partials closely paired), many edge dipole segments and loops, APBII residual fringes, dislocation reactions forming sessile segments
truth.structures.sample_35al.overall_structure: As-cast polycrystalline B2 ordered structure with very weak DO3 order, containing superdislocations.
truth.structures.sample_35al.microstructure_li: Straight near-screw superdislocations (two 1/2<111> partials very close), many edge dipole segments and loops, weak APBII residual fringes

prediction.structures.sample_15al.overall_structure: Disordered bcc Fe-Al with single dislocations arranged in loose cellular distribution
prediction.structures.sample_15al.microstructure_li: single dislocations and dipoles in incipient cell structure
prediction.structures.sample_20al.overall_structure: Disordered/short-range ordered bcc Fe-Al with curled single dislocations and dipoles
prediction.structures.sample_20al.microstructure_li: curled single dislocations of Burgers vector (1/2)<111>, some straight screw dislocations, edge dipole segments and small loops formed by trailing from gliding screw dislocations
prediction.structures.sample_25al.overall_structure: DO3 ordered Fe-Al with straight near-screw dislocations, edge dipoles, and loosely coupled dislocations trailing APB faults
prediction.structures.sample_25al.microstructure_li: DO3 ordered Fe-Al with coarse B2 domains
straight near-screw dislocations, edge dipole segments, loosely coupled screw dipoles, APBI and APBII faults; dislocations largely glide as individual (1/2)<111> dislocations
prediction.structures.sample_28al.overall_structure: Ordered Fe-Al near work hardening peak with straight superdislocations, dipoles, and sessile dislocation locks
prediction.structures.sample_28al.microstructure_li: straight near-screw superdislocations composed of two (1/2)<111> partials trailing APBII faults; superdislocation dipoles; sessile edge <100> dislocation locks produced by reactions of glissile <111> superdislocations
prediction.structures.sample_30al.overall_structure: Weakly DO3 ordered Fe-Al with closely coupled B2 superdislocations, edge dipoles, and stepped structures from dislocation reactions
prediction.structures.sample_30al.microstructure_li: superdislocations of two (1/2)<111> partials with small separation due to high APBI energy; straight near-screw segments; stepped structures from dislocation reactions; edge dipoles and loops; APBII fringes from movement destroying DO3 order
prediction.structures.sample_35al.overall_structure: B2 ordered Fe-Al with closely coupled superdislocations and numerous edge dipole segments
prediction.structures.sample_35al.microstructure_li: B2 ordered Fe-Al with some DO3 order
straight near-screw B2 superdislocations (two closely coupled (1/2)<111> partials due to high APBI); edge dipole segments dragged from moving screw superdislocations; weak remnant APBII fringes

truth.interfaces.sample_25al.defect_interactio: Single 1/2<111> dislocations trail APBI faults, loosely coupled
truth.interfaces.sample_25al.phase_evolution: APB faults created by dislocation movement in DO3 ordered material.
truth.interfaces.sample_28al.defect_interactio: Superdislocation pairs trailing APBII, forming sessile locks
Pairs of 1/2<111> dislocations trail APBII faults; reactions produce sessile <100> edge dislocations.
truth.interfaces.sample_28al.phase_evolution: Movement of superdislocations destroys weak DO3 order, creating APBII faults.
truth.interfaces.sample_30al.defect_interactio: Pairs of 1/2<111> partials destroy weak DO3 order, leaving APBII fringes.
truth.interfaces.sample_35al.defect_interactio: Superdislocation motion causing weak APBII contrast
Pairs of 1/2<111> partials destroy residual DO3 order, leaving weak fringes.
truth.interfaces.sample_35al.phase_evolution: Minor DO3 order destroyed by deformation.

prediction.interfaces.sample_28al.defect_interactio: Two glissile superdislocations with different <111> Burgers vectors on different {110} glide planes react to produce a pair of edge <100> dislocations sitting in sessile configuration on {001} planes, e.g., [111] + [11-1] forming [100] edge along [010] in (001), constituting strong barriers to further room temperature superdislocation motion.
prediction.interfaces.sample_30al.defect_interactio: Reactions between dislocations lead to stepped structures; visible dislocation pairs are the result of reactions, not the partials of glissile superdislocations.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_25al.key: Loose pairs of single dislocations trailing APB faults, many near-screw dipoles; thermal APBs and DO3 domain contrast observed.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_28al.tec: TEM, bright field, ±g imaging, weak beam
truth.characterization_methods.sample_28al.pur: Analysis of superdislocation dipoles and reaction locks
truth.characterization_methods.sample_28al.key: Straight near-screw superdislocations (two 1/2<111> partials), many dipole pairs, formation of sessile <100> edge dislocations by reactions.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_30al.pur: Imaging of superdislocation structures and APB contrast
truth.characterization_methods.sample_30al.key: Closely paired superdislocations, many edge dipole segments, APBII residual fringes, dislocation reactions forming stepped structures.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_35al.pur: Observation of superdislocation dipoles and APB contrast
truth.characterization_methods.sample_35al.key: Straight near-screw superdislocations with very close partials, many edge dipole segments; weak APBII fringes from destroyed DO3 order.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_15al.pur: Examine dislocation structures, Burgers vectors, and planar faults after room temperature compression
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_15al.key: Single dislocations arranged in a loose cellular distribution; many dipole segments in clustered regions; zone axis near [013], imaging g vector 200.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_20al.pur: Examine dislocation structures, Burgers vectors, and planar faults after room temperature compression
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_20al.key: Curled single dislocations of Burgers vector (1/2)<111> showing dipole arrangements and loops; some straight screw dislocations with narrow edge dipoles and small loops; zone axes near [011] and [001], g vectors 011 and 110.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_25al.pur: Examine dislocation structures, Burgers vectors, APB faults, and domain structures after room temperature compression
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_25al.key: Straight near-screw dislocations with edge dipole segments and loosely coupled screw dipoles; APBI and APBII faults visible; thermal APBs from coarse B2 domains and fine DO3 domain speckling; dislocations glide largely as individual (1/2)<111>; zone axis near [001], g vectors 100, 200, and 110.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_28al.pur: Examine dislocation structures, Burgers vectors, superdislocation configurations, and reactions after room temperature compression
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_28al.key: Many straight superdislocations composed of two (1/2)<111> partials in near-screw orientations along {110} planes; numerous dipole pairs and non-dipole pairs; sessile edge <100> dislocation locks produced by reactions between glissile <111> superdislocations; zone axis near [001], g vectors 200 and 110; weak beam near g:3-4g.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_30al.pur: Examine dislocation structures, Burgers vectors, and planar faults after room temperature compression
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_30al.key: Superdislocations of two (1/2)<111> partials with small separation due to high APBI energy; straight near-screw segments and stepped structures from dislocation reactions; many edge dipoles and loops; background APBII fringes from movement destroying weak DO3 order; zone axes near [111] and [001], g vector 110.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_35al.pur: Examine dislocation structures, Burgers vectors, and planar faults after room temperature compression
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_35al.key: Straight near-screw B2 superdislocations with closely coupled (1/2)<111> partials due to high APBI energy; many edge dipole segments, some dragged from moving screw superdislocations; weak remnant APBII fringes; zone axes near [110] and [113], g vector 110.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Work hardening rate peaks in the range 26–29 at.% Al, maximum at ~28% Al.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The high work hardening is explained by the combination of superdislocation dipole hardening and the predominance of straight screw dislocations due to strong Peierls (core) effects.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Elastic anisotropy peaks near Fe–25–30% Al, but the authors argue that Peierls forces, not anisotropy itself, are responsible for the screw dislocation alignment and high work hardening.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Addition of 0.1% B caused a slight increase in flow stress but no consistent change in work hardening rate.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: A small ductility peak at ~28% Al is attributed to the high work hardening rate meeting the Considère criterion.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Fig. 3 APB energy data from Ray–Crawford–Cockayne: APBI in DO3 order decreases from 78 to 65 mJ/m² over 25.5–28% Al, APBII 78–65 mJ/m² over 26–29% Al, APBI in B2 increases from 65 to 110 mJ/m² over 31–36% Al.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Fig. 4 elastic stiffness data show C44 increase and (C11-C12)/2 decrease near 25–30% Al, leading to high Zener anisotropy factor A.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Dislocation reactions forming sessile <100> edge dislocations were observed in Fe–28% Al and Fe–30% Al, contributing to additional hardening.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fig. 1 shows that yield stress increases steadily with Al content up to 25% Al, followed by a rapid fall at 25–28% Al and little change or a slight increase over 30–40% Al; ductility shows an essentially inverse relationship with a rapid fall to 25% Al, a slight maximum at 28% Al, and a steady slow fall thereafter. Data are from thermally-relaxed as-cast materials with coarse grain sizes and no solute or precipitate effects.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Fig. 2 shows work hardening rate measured as the secant over 1–1.5% plastic strain: low for disordered Fe-Al with less than 20% Al, a sharp peak in the 26–29% Al range (especially 28–29% Al), and a moderate value above about 35% Al. Similar trends are seen over strain ranges 0.5–1% and 1–2%. Two data points correspond to Fe-Al alloys containing about 0.1% B, which show little consistent change in work hardening rate relative to B-free alloys.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fig. 3 gives APB energies from literature: APBI in DO3 order decreases from about 78 mJ/m^2 at 25.5% Al to 65 mJ/m^2 at 28% Al; APBI in B2 order is about 65 mJ/m^2 at 31% Al rising to 110 mJ/m^2 at 36% Al; APBII in DO3 order decreases from about 78 mJ/m^2 at 26% Al to 65 mJ/m^2 at 29% Al.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Fig. 4 shows elastic stiffness constants and Zener anisotropy factor A = 2C44/(C11-C12); Fe3Al alloys exhibit extremely high elastic anisotropy due to both an increase in C44 and a fall in (C11-C12)/2.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The peak in work hardening rate near 28% Al is attributed to two main factors: (i) the presence of superdislocations which produce stronger superdislocation dipole locks than single dislocation dipoles, and (ii) the increased tendency of dislocations to lie in straight screw orientations due to strong Peierls forces. The coincidence with high elastic anisotropy is believed to reflect interatomic forces generating strong Peierls effects, rather than elastic anisotropy being the direct cause.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: No APB tubes were observed in any deformed material, providing little support for the APB-tube-dragging model of work hardening.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: No evidence of the operation of coupled four-partial dislocations (which would constitute the perfect superdislocation for the DO3 ordered state) was found in any material, including Fe-25% Al where APB energies are too low to maintain coupled superdislocations.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: In Fe-28% Al, detailed Burgers vector analysis confirms a reaction of two superdislocations with different <111> Burgers vectors on different {110} planes producing a pair of edge <100> dislocations on a (001) plane, e.g., [111] + [11-1] -> [100] edge along [010], constituting sessile locks that prevent further room temperature superdislocation motion.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The small ductility peak observed at about 28% Al is explained by the Considere criterion as arising from the peak in work hardening rate over the 26–30% Al composition range where the flow stress is relatively constant.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Grain size for all cast materials is of the order of 0.25 mm, ensuring no significant grain size effect on strength.
